name: 2016 ACR/EULAR Classification Criteria for Primary Sjögren's Syndrome
classification_rule: Target: patients with at least one symptom of ocular/oral dryness, OR suspicion from ESSDAI questionnaire. Weighted scoring; total ≥4 = classify as primary Sjögren's. Exclusion of relevant mimics required.
target_population: At least one symptom of ocular or oral dryness (per AECG inclusion criteria) OR
Suspected Sjögren's from the ESSDAI systemic disease questionnaire
exclusions: Active hepatitis C infection (confirmed by PCR)

weighted_items.label: Labial salivary gland with focal lymphocytic sialadenitis and focus score ≥1 focus/4 mm²
Ocular staining score ≥5 (or van Bijsterveld ≥4) in at least one eye
Schirmer test ≤5 mm/5 min in at least one eye
Unstimulated whole salivary flow rate ≤0.1 mL/min

todo: Encode the eligibility + exclusion gates and the weighted scorer; wire the systemic disease activity index (ESSDAI) for organ-domain scoring on Day 2.

references.title: 2016 American College of Rheumatology/European League Against Rheumatism classification criteria for primary Sjögren's syndrome
references.authors: Shiboski CH, Shiboski SC, Seror R, et al.
references.why_it_matters: Current reference classification. Consolidates 2002 AECG and 2012 ACR criteria. Sensitivity 96%, specificity 95%. Biopsy and anti-SSA/Ro each weighted 3 — either alone with one ocular/oral objective test gives classification.

references.title: Primary Sjögren's syndrome
references.why_it_matters: Current NEJM clinical-practice review. Covers pathogenesis, systemic manifestations (interstitial lung disease, renal tubular acidosis, neuropathy, lymphoma risk), and evidence-based management.

references.title: Sjögren syndrome
references.authors: Brito-Zerón P, Baldini C, Bootsma H, et al.
references.why_it_matters: Comprehensive Nature Primer covering epidemiology, mechanism, clinical phenotypes, diagnosis, and management including 40× increased risk of B-cell NHL (MALT).

references.title: EULAR recommendations for the management of Sjögren's syndrome with topical and systemic therapies
references.authors: Ramos-Casals M, Brito-Zerón P, Bombardieri S, et al.
references.why_it_matters: Current EULAR guidance on topical (artificial tears, cholinergics, cyclosporine drops) and systemic (hydroxychloroquine, MTX, MMF, rituximab for severe systemic disease) therapy.

references.title: Rheumatoid factor and disease activity are independent predictors of lymphoma in primary Sjögren's syndrome
references.authors: Nocturne G, Virone A, Ng W-F, et al.
references.why_it_matters: Multicentre cohort identifying rheumatoid factor and ESSDAI disease activity as independent predictors of lymphoma in primary Sjögren's. Critical follow-up driver when classification is confirmed — clusters with persistent parotid enlargement, low C4, cryoglobulinaemia.
